LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED IN PART. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOLLOWING AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES: (21) PRIVILEGE AND JUSTIFICATION - THE ANSWER FAILS TO SPECIFY WHICH CAUSES OF ACTION THIS AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE APPLIES TO AS REQUIRED BY CRC 2.112; AND (23) FRAUD - THE ANSWER DOES NOT PLEAD FRAUD WITH THE REQUISITE PARTICULARITY. THE DEMURRER TO THE FOLLOWING AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND: (46) FAILURE TO STATE REPRESENTATIVE B&P CLAIM - THERE ARE NO CLASS OR REPRESENTATIVE ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT AND THEREFORE THIS AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE IS INAPPLICABLE. THE DEMURRER TO ALL OTHER AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES IS OVERRULED. THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: (36) ABSENCE OF DISABILITY - STRICKEN AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION; (37) FAILURE TO COMMUNICATE DISABILITY - STRICKEN AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION; (39) UNDUE BURDEN - STRICKEN AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION; AND (42) FRIVOLOUS, UNREASONABLE AND LACKING FOUNDATION - STRICKEN AS TO THE 5TH THROUGH 12TH CAUSES OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS AUSTIN EDWARDS HILLS 2004 TRUST AND HILLS VINEYARD,INC'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE TO NAPA COUNTY IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT MET THEIR BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING THAT TRAVELING LESS THAN 50 MILES FROM NAPA TO SAN FRANCISCO WOULD BE TOO INCONVENIENT FOR THIRD PARTY WITNESSES. (SEE HAMILTON V. SUPERIOR COURT (1974) 37 CAL.APP.3D 418, 424, "THE BURDEN RESTS ON THE ONE WHO SEEKS A CHANGE OF VENUE'TO PROVE THAT THE CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES AND ENDS OF JUSTICE WILL BE PROMOTED THEREBY, AND THIS HE MUST DO THROUGH AFFIDAVITS THAT CONTAIN MORE THAN GENERALITIES AND CONCLUSIONS.") THE ONLY EVIDENCE SUBMITTED WITH DEFENDANTS' MOVING PAPERS ARE THREE DECLARATIONS - ONE FROM DEFENDANT'S PRESIDENT, ONE FROM DEFENDANT'S PRESIDENT'S PERSONAL ASSISTANT, AND ONE FROM DEFENDANTS' ATTORNEY. NONE OF THESE DECLARATIONS SET FORTH ANY FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT IT WOULD BE TOO INCONVENIENT FOR THIRD PARTY WITNESSES TO TRAVEL TO SAN FRANCISCO. THE COURT DECLINES TO CONSIDER THE DECLARATIONS SUBMITTED FOR THE FIRST TIME IN REPLY, AS DEFENDANTS MAKE NO SHOWING OF WHY THIS EVIDENCE WAS NOT AVAILABLE AT THE TIME THE MOVING PAPERS WERE FILED. (JAY V. MAHAFFEY (2013) 218 CAL.APP.4TH 1522, 1537, "THE GENERAL RULE OF MOTION PRACTICE, WHICH APPLIES HERE, IS THAT NEW EVIDENCE IS NOT PERMITTED WITH REPLY PAPERS'DEFENDANTS' DECISION TO WAIT UNTIL THE REPLY BRIEFS TO BRING FORTH ANY EVIDENCE AT ALL, WHEN THE [PLAINTIFFS] WOULD HAVE NO OPPORTUNITY TO RESPOND, WAS SIMPLY UNFAIR.") IN ANY EVENT, EVEN IF THE COURT WERE TO CONSIDER THE DECLARATIONS SUBMITTED IN REPLY, THEY WOULD NOT CHANGE THE RESULT. THE VAST MAJORITY OF THE DECLARATIONS ARE FROM DEFENDANTS' EMPLOYEES. CONSIDERATION IS NOT GIVEN TO THE CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES WHO ARE EMPLOYEES OF THE LITIGANTS. (STUTE V. BURINDA (1981) 123 CAL.APP.3D SUPP. 11, 105-106.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-13-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF MANUEL TORRES' DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is overruled in part and sustained in part. The motion to strike is granted in part. The demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the following affirmative defenses: (21) privilege and justification - the answer fails to specify which causes of action this affirmative defense applies to as required by CRC 2.112; and (23) fraud - the answer does not plead fraud with the requisite particularity. The demurrer to the following affirmative defense is sustained without leave to amend: (46) failure to state representative B&P claim - there are no class or representative allegations in the complaint and therefore this affirmative defense is inapplicable. The demurrer to all other affirmative defenses is overruled. The motion to strike is granted as follows: (36) absence of disability - stricken as to the 4th cause of action; (37) failure to communicate disability - stricken as to the 4th cause of action; (39) undue burden - stricken as to the 4th cause of action; and (42) frivolous, unreasonable and lacking foundation - stricken as to the 5th through 12th causes of action. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-12-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT MANUEL TORRES'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT OF HILLS VINEYARD, INC. AND AUSTIN EDWARDS HILLS 2004 TRUST is overruled. The Cross-Complaint does not allege a breach of the severance agreement, but rather a breach of an oral and/or implied agreement between the parties that if Plaintiff did not accept the severance offer made by Defendant, Plaintiff would return the money given to him by Defendant. (Cross-Compl. 3, 4.) Thus, there is no basis for the Court to consider the documents that are allegedly part of the severance offer. Additionally, the Cross-Complaint sufficiently alleges each element of the three remaining causes of action. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Change Venue
Off Calendar Sep-18-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS AUSTIN EDWARDS HILLS 2004 TRUST and HILLS VINEYARD,INC's Motion To Change Venue to Napa County is denied. Defendants have not met their burden of establishing that traveling less than 50 miles from Napa to San Francisco would be too inconvenient for third party witnesses. (See Hamilton v. Superior Court (1974) 37 Cal.App.3d 418, 424, "The burden rests on the one who seeks a change of venue'to prove that the convenience of witnesses and ends of justice will be promoted thereby, and this he must do through affidavits that contain more than generalities and conclusions.") The only evidence submitted with Defendants' moving papers are three declarations - one from Defendant's President, one from Defendant's President's personal assistant, and one from Defendants' attorney. None of these declarations set forth any facts establishing that it would be too inconvenient for third party witnesses to travel to San Francisco. The Court declines to consider the declarations submitted for the first time in reply, as Defendants make no showing of why this evidence was not available at the time the moving papers were filed. (Jay v. Mahaffey (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1522, 1537, "The general rule of motion practice, which applies here, is that new evidence is not permitted with reply papers'Defendants' decision to wait until the reply briefs to bring forth any evidence at all, when the [plaintiffs] would have no opportunity to respond, was simply unfair.") In any event, even if the Court were to consider the declarations submitted in reply, they would not change the result. The vast majority of the declarations are from Defendants' employees. Consideration is not given to the convenience of witnesses who are employees of the litigants. (Stute v. Burinda (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d Supp. 11, 105-106.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976
